title: Podman 6: Automatic BoltDB to SQLite migration
body:
@Luap99 I don't *think* we are vulnerable to the issues we have in 5.8 as there is no possibility of ever getting a BoltDB backend for Podman, but eyes on this would be appreciated.

This is an alternative to https://github.com/chttps://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28553ontainers/podman/pull/28553 and, instead of warning folks to use an external binary or downgrade, adds just enough BoltDB back in to do a migration.

The bad news: I don't think we can actually test this in CI. The ability to make new Bolt databases, and to interact with them in a meaningful way, is entirely gone. Though, now that I think about it, maybe I can ship a Bolt database in the tests directory and do a test migration off it?

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
The BoltDB to SQLite migration possible in 5.8, which was planned to be removed in 6.0, has been left in place to ensure users are still able to recover their data if they move from Podman 5.7 or earlier to Podman 6.0 without stopping at Podman 5.8 for a migration.
```

comments.body: tmt tests failed for commit b36dad653eacd381890ce100fea5f6b242faa393. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.
tmt tests failed for commit 5301b8c015850fb019fa77e53a7e095c118fb870. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.
@mheon tests aren't happy, most notably the FreeBSD cross
tmt tests failed for commit 17d11c3c63e02f037dcd65eaad82efcb8c04f985. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

Alright. I am abandoning the hope of automatic testing. Reasoning:

I can ship a BoltDB database for testing (as we can't create them anymore). I can ship `podman inspect` output for the new/old objects for comparison (since we cant't inspect using BoltDB anymore). I cannot validate that the BoltDB inspect matches the SQLite inspect because the Bolt inspect comes from 5.8 and the SQLite from 6.0, and we've already made breaking changes in that direction - and we'll keep adding more fields in the future. Comparing `podman inspect` output before/after was the best way we had of validating the old container and migrated container matched, and if we can't do it, I don't see value in integration tests for this.

I have manually validated root/rootless migrations locally.

Uhm, so actually testing this, this is like broken in the most basic way?! 

The way this is done in getDBState() just bypasses all your migration code if we are not in refresh, i.e. if a users updates to 6.0 and runs any podman command before rebooting they all just default to sqlite. And even people who reboot will trigger podman commands most likely due podman container cleanup which gets triggered on shutdown.

Do we want to hard-error in the migration-needed-but-not-performed case? I updated the branch so it does warn you now, but I could easily swap that over to an error

> Do we want to hard-error in the migration-needed-but-not-performed case? I updated the branch so it does warn you now, but I could easily swap that over to an error

Well what does the warning do? Warn and use sqlite corrupts the state that new objects will be in sqlite while old ones in boltdb which cannot ever be allowed?
Damn it, broke it so it can't actually migrate

/hold

Need to do some reworking of runtime init logic, so we know if we're going to be trying a migration before we get the database

/hold cancel

Alright, fixed up and should be working now. In the process uncovered some pretty significant bugs with how we do runtime init.
